jwcListedAreas.latestUpdate: JWC circular JWLA-033 (3 March 2026) added Bahrain, Djibouti, Kuwait, Oman and Qatar to listed areas and amended the broader Persian/Arabian Gulf, Gulf of Oman, Indian Ocean, Gulf of Aden and Southern Red Sea zone. No areas have been removed since.

premiumReadings.level: around 1% of hull value per voyage
premiumReadings.source: S&P Global Commodity Insights; Marsh McLennan; Reuters (Mar–Jun 2026)

premiumReadings.level: higher and volatile; quoted as high as ~3% during peak March tension, ~0.8% on successful transits after no-claims adjustments

editorialReading: Cover remains available in the London market — the Lloyd's Market Association reports war-risk insurance is still being written — but safety concerns are thinning vessel traffic through Hormuz, and pricing now turns voyage by voyage. The early-June Israeli strike on Iran's Mahshahr petrochemical complex, the first hit on Iranian energy infrastructure since the April ceasefire, has put a direct energy-asset risk premium back on top of the chokepoint premium. As our Beyond the Strait analysis argued, the structural risk floor is visible here: cover hasn't withdrawn, but its terms have hardened in ways that don't symmetrically reset on a ceasefire headline. Hormuz may reopen in form before it reopens in function — limited, negotiated transits are not the same as restored commercial confidence.
watchNext: The signals that matter now: a formal US–Iran arrangement on Hormuz (Washington's top priority, Tehran's main leverage); confirmation or denial of Iranian/Omani transit fees; any further Israeli strikes on Iranian energy assets after Mahshahr; Hezbollah's response to the fragile Lebanon ceasefire; renewed Houthi threats in the Red Sea; Saudi export volumes after the July OSP cut; and EU jet-fuel coordination. A further LMA/JWC Listed Area circular (a possible JWLA-034) would confirm a fresh hardening.
methodologyNote: Premium ranges aggregated from publicly-cited figures in news sources; exact rates are confidential between brokers and underwriters. JWC Listed Areas from Lloyd's Joint War Committee circular JWLA-033 (3 March 2026). Editorial reading is our market interpretation, not a republished source.
